message:
feat(doctor_frankentui): cancellation-aware deadline and AGENTS.md crate inventory update

- Add CancellationToken integration to Deadline for cooperative shutdown
- Add ftui-runtime dependency for cancellation support
- Expand AGENTS.md with doctor_frankentui, ftui-a11y crate entries
- Update beads issue tracker

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-mn4a0","title":"task(doctor-perf): eliminate duplicate subprocess and comparison work in high-cost doctor paths","description":"Background:\nRecent review work already showed how easy it is for capture/observe paths to duplicate app launches or split observation from the intended target. More broadly, doctor workflows can burn large amounts of time by redundantly launching, comparing, or re-deriving information that should be shared.\n\nGoal:\nImplement the first doctor optimization lever by removing provably redundant workflow work while preserving artifact quality and replayability.","design":"Work packages:\n- Use the workflow cost map to identify the highest-value redundant subprocess or comparison patterns.\n- Keep the implementation centered on one primary redundancy-elimination lever at a time.\n- Require explicit reasoning about how the change preserves captured evidence, summary correctness, and replay commands.\n- Define conservative fallback behavior when the deduplication conditions are not met.\n- Record what before/after artifacts should prove the savings and the preserved semantics.","acceptance_criteria":"1. The bead has a tight scope centered on removing redundant work, not broadly making doctor faster.
2. Evidence quality and replayability are explicit review criteria.
3. The task text gives future reviewers enough context to judge whether a proposed implementation stayed aligned with project goals.
4. The implementation is incomplete without focused unit/integration tests plus scripted failure-path E2E evidence and detailed logs proving when deduplication did or did not apply.","notes":"This bead should favor clarity over cleverness. A slightly smaller win that preserves operator trust is better than an aggressive dedupe strategy that makes failures harder to understand.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T18:46:34.449422623Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-29T02:44:42.755923604Z","closed_at":"2026-03-29T02:44:42.755611149Z","close_reason":"Completed: deduplicated suite/report run_meta loading, added proof logs, and expanded tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-artifact-coding","diagnostics","doctor_frankentui","extreme-software-optimization","ftui-performance-control-plane","performance","process"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-mn4a0","depends_on_id":"bd-dyr5a","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T19:00:30.627460470Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-mn4a0","depends_on_id":"bd-js3c3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T19:00:27.614924008Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-mn4a0","depends_on_id":"bd-qbbkv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T19:00:24.626317771Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-muv6p","title":"task(perf-foundation): build deterministic macrobenchmark and replay fixture suites","description":"Background:\nThe program needs repeatable workloads that are representative of real use rather than tiny microbenchmarks. These fixtures should exercise render-heavy screens, runtime stress paths, and doctor workflows in ways that can be replayed consistently and compared across revisions.\n\nGoal:\nCreate the canonical fixture suites and replay assets used by baseline capture, profiling, parity checks, and tail-latency gauntlets.","design":"Work packages:
- Translate the inventory bead into concrete fixture families for render, runtime, and doctor lanes.
- Partition fixtures into canonical regression suites and adversarial/challenge suites so the project gets both deterministic repeatability and robustness coverage.
- Ensure fixtures capture representative state transitions such as sparse updates, large invalidations, resize churn, subscription churn, cancellations, timeouts, degraded I/O, and artifact-heavy diagnostic runs.
- Define reproducibility rules: seeds, timing controls, host fingerprints, and fixture versioning.
- Specify artifact naming so baseline/profile/replay/challenge data can be compared over time.
- Include explicit notes on why each fixture exists, which user/operator pain it approximates, and whether it is expected to demonstrate wins, graceful fallback, or negative-control stability.","acceptance_criteria":"1. The project has deterministic fixtures that future performance work can reuse without reinventing workloads.
2. Each fixture is traceable to an explicit optimization question or invariant from the inventory bead.
3. Fixture metadata is rich enough to support profiling, shadow comparison, and later rollout scorecards.
4. The suite explicitly includes adversarial challenge fixtures and negative-control cases so optimizations cannot quietly overfit to a narrow curated benchmark set.","notes":"Planning correction: the fixture layer now explicitly distinguishes canonical regression fixtures from challenge fixtures. That protects users from benchmark-specialized optimizations that would otherwise look excellent in curated runs but regress rarer real-world regimes.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T18:46:24.344944735Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-18T02:13:12.218077994Z","closed_at":"2026-03-18T02:13:12.217570453Z","close_reason":"Implemented fixture_suite.rs","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-artifact-coding","benchmarking","extreme-software-optimization","fixtures","ftui-performance-control-plane","observability","performance","performance-foundation"]}

 {"id":"bd-npalh","title":"Tests: perf budget + log assertions","description":"Goal
- Validate performance thresholds and structured log content.

Acceptance
- Failing budgets produce detailed logs with sample/mode/metrics.
","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"subtask","created_at":"2026-02-05T15:04:36.589787057Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T17:30:27.214004703Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T17:30:27.213986509Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo-showcase","mermaid","subtask","tests"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-npalh","depends_on_id":"bd-zc0d7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:34Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":980,"issue_id":"bd-npalh","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented 8 tests in mermaid_perf_budget.rs:

**Timing budget tests** (release-mode only, skip in debug):
- mermaid_pipeline_budget_120x40: full parse→layout→render pipeline budget per sample at 120x40 (123ms CI budget)
- mermaid_pipeline_budget_80x24: same at 80x24 (100ms CI budget)
- mermaid_sample_switch_budget: navigation + re-render timing (150ms budget)

**Quality/correctness tests**:
- mermaid_all_samples_render_cleanly: all samples render without panics at 80x24/120x40/200x60
- mermaid_cache_hit_faster_than_miss: cache hit is faster than cache miss

**JSONL log assertion tests** (PTY-based):
- mermaid_jsonl_render_event_complete_schema: validates all 10 required + 12 metric fields in mermaid_render events
- mermaid_jsonl_all_samples_logged: verifies all parseable samples produce render events
- mermaid_jsonl_metric_values_sane: validates crossings ≤20, compactness ≥0.01 (flowchart-type), symmetry 0.0-1.0, errors ≤5

All failure messages include sample name, mode, and metrics for actionable diagnostics. Quality gates clean (fmt, clippy, all tests pass).","created_at":"2026-02-05T17:30:23Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-vb2b7","title":"feature(doctor-perf): optimize doctor_frankentui orchestration and evidence economy","description":"Background:\ndoctor_frankentui is both a performance consumer and a confidence engine. It launches subprocesses, captures evidence, writes artifacts, and helps operators understand failures. Performance work here must therefore reduce redundant work without weakening evidence quality or replayability.\n\nGoal:\nProfile and optimize the most expensive doctor workflows while preserving or improving the quality of summaries, manifests, replay helpers, and failure triage artifacts.","design":"Feature scope:\n- Profile workflow-level cost centers across capture, replay, report, and failure handling.\n- Remove duplicate subprocess, artifact, and comparison work.\n- Tighten artifact contracts so evidence stays rich but no longer bloated or repetitive.\n- Validate with soak, shadow, and deterministic replay suites.\n\nGuiding principle:\nThe output of doctor work must stay useful to humans during failure, not merely become cheaper to compute.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Optimization decisions are grounded in workflow-level evidence rather than isolated function timings.
2. Artifact quality, replayability, and operator clarity remain first-class acceptance criteria.
3. The resulting doctor lane improves both throughput and the usability of failure evidence.
4. The lane is incomplete without focused unit/integration coverage, scripted failure-path and soak E2E runs, and detailed logging/manifests that explain every mismatch or fallback.","notes":"Doctor work is easy to accidentally optimize in the wrong direction by deleting valuable evidence or making failure modes less intelligible. The bead text therefore makes evidence economy, not artifact austerity, the target.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-14T18:46:22.840986034Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-30T02:56:27.596681359Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-artifact-coding","diagnostics","doctor_frankentui","extreme-software-optimization","ftui-performance-control-plane","performance"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vb2b7","depends_on_id":"bd-mn4a0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T18:58:24.291330399Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-vb2b7","depends_on_id":"bd-qbbkv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T18:58:21.280349810Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-vb2b7","depends_on_id":"bd-tsgb4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T18:58:27.297874255Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-vb2b7","depends_on_id":"bd-zmcvz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T18:58:30.303349902Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-vcq4","title":"Advanced Text Editor: Undo history panel + shortcuts","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T07:41:39.337226162Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T07:41:44.403503036Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T07:41:44.403483710Z","close_reason":"Implemented undo/redo history panel, shortcuts, status indicator, and tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-vins","title":"Text Effects: Character Reveal Modes","description":"## Background
The typewriter effect is limited to left-to-right reveal. A comprehensive reveal system enables dramatic text appearances for titles, announcements, and onboarding.

## Implementation

Add RevealMode enum (used as parameter, not nested in TextEffect):
```rust
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, Default)]
pub enum RevealMode {
    #[default]
    LeftToRight,    // Classic typewriter
    RightToLeft,    // Reverse typewriter  
    CenterOut,      // Expand from middle
    EdgesIn,        // Converge to center
    Random,         // Random order (deterministic with seed)
    ByWord,         // Word at a time
    ByLine,         // Line at a time (for multi-line)
}
```

Add TextEffect variant (consistent structure with others):
```rust
TextEffect::Reveal {
    mode: RevealMode,
    progress: f64,      // 0.0 = hidden, 1.0 = fully revealed
    seed: u64,          // For Random mode determinism
}

// Separate variant for gradient mask (different parameters)
TextEffect::RevealMask {
    angle: f64,         // Sweep angle in degrees (0 = left-to-right)
    progress: f64,      // Mask position 0.0-1.0
    softness: f64,      // Edge softness 0.0 (hard) to 1.0 (gradient)
}
```

### Visibility Calculation by Mode:

| Mode | Visible When |
|------|--------------|
| LeftToRight | char_idx < progress * total_chars |
| RightToLeft | char_idx >= (1-progress) * total_chars |
| CenterOut | distance_from_center < progress * max_distance |
| EdgesIn | distance_from_edge < progress * max_distance |
| Random | hash(seed, idx) < progress * total_chars |
| ByWord | word_idx < progress * total_words |
| ByLine | line_idx < progress * total_lines |
| RevealMask | rotated_position > threshold (with softness fade) |

### Usage:
```rust
// Simple left-to-right reveal
StyledText::new("Hello World")
    .effect(TextEffect::Reveal { 
        mode: RevealMode::LeftToRight, 
        progress: 0.5,
        seed: 0,
    })

// Dramatic gradient mask sweep
StyledText::new("WELCOME")
    .effect(TextEffect::RevealMask {
        angle: 45.0,
        progress: 0.5,
        softness: 0.3,
    })
```

## Files
crates/ftui-extras/src/text_effects.rs

## Unit Tests (REQUIRED)
- test_reveal_ltr_first_chars_first: progress=0.5 shows first half
- test_reveal_rtl_last_chars_first: progress=0.5 shows last half
- test_reveal_center_out_middle_first: Center chars visible first
- test_reveal_edges_in_edges_first: Edge chars visible first
- test_reveal_random_deterministic: Same seed = same reveal order
- test_reveal_random_all_chars_reveal: progress=1 shows all
- test_reveal_by_word_whole_words: Partial progress shows complete words
- test_reveal_by_line_whole_lines: Lines reveal atomically
- test_reveal_mask_angle_0: Left-to-right sweep
- test_reveal_mask_angle_90: Top-to-bottom sweep  
- test_reveal_mask_softness_0: Hard edge (binary visible/hidden)
- test_reveal_mask_softness_1: Gradient fade across chars

## Visual Tests
- Snapshot CenterOut at progress 0.25, 0.5, 0.75
- Snapshot RevealMask at angle=45, softness=0.3

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Text Effects: Character Reveal Modes
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode

Logging: JSONL with action, effect, frame index, hash, dims, seed, FPS estimate, and input sequence markers.

Considerations: Deterministic inputs and seeds; verify both alt-screen and inline modes.","acceptance_criteria":"1) Script runs for Doom and Quake in alt + inline at 120x40 (and 80x24).
2) JSONL logs capture per-frame hashes and input events.
3) Hashes stable across runs with fixed input sequence.","notes":"Progress: added effect/mode/size env overrides to scripts/demo_visual_fx_e2e.sh; added tests/e2e/scripts/test_visual_fx_fps.sh wrapper (runs Doom/Quake only) and wired into tests/e2e/scripts/run_all.sh.

Update 2026-02-05: switched scripts/demo_visual_fx_e2e.sh to use pty_run + pty metadata to avoid "No such device or address" on headless runs; added JSONL schema support for type=case (schema + examples + validator). Latest run log dir: /tmp/ftui_e2e_logs_run_1770265051/visual_fx_fps; summary in /tmp/ftui_e2e_results_run_1770265051/summary.json.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"codex","created_at":"2026-02-04T18:14:48.855862806Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T04:18:42.596597443Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T04:14:42.282001712Z","close_reason":"E2E Doom/Quake VFX script passed in alt+inline at 80x24/120x40 with JSONL + hash validation; logs under /tmp/ftui_e2e_logs/visual_fx_fps_run2","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vmuax","depends_on_id":"bd-22n80","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:34Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-vmuax","depends_on_id":"bd-3isi7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:34Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-vtor0","title":"task(render-perf): map the end-to-end cost surface of view -> buffer -> diff -> present -> write","description":"Background:\nThe render pipeline is where the clean architectural seams offer the biggest opportunity, but we need to know which stages dominate under which fixtures before deciding whether to target view invalidation, buffer writes, diff work, presenter state churn, or terminal writes.\n\nGoal:\nProduce the definitive stage-level render cost map that informs every later render optimization bead.","design":"Work packages:
- Reuse the existing reusable-buffer diff+present macrobenchmark work for demo screens as the seed benchmark path rather than creating a separate render benchmark universe.
- Break down render costs by stage, workload, and update shape.
- Track not only wall-clock time but also touched cells, changed regions, emitted bytes, and other stage-relevant counters.
- Identify workload classes where sparse-change assumptions hold and those where they fail.
- Highlight whether average cost or tail spikes are the bigger problem in each stage.
- Explain which findings point toward work-elimination opportunities versus low-level inner-loop tuning.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Later render beads can cite a concrete cost map instead of guessing which stage matters most.\n2. The bead explicitly distinguishes scenarios where skip certificates are likely high-EV from scenarios where they are not.\n3. The resulting analysis is detailed enough to justify prioritization between diff, presenter, and cache-related work.","notes":"This bead is where render optimization stops being generic advice and becomes FrankenTUI-specific engineering.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T18:46:27.136253085Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-18T02:20:59.273538727Z","closed_at":"2026-03-18T02:20:59.273000348Z","close_reason":"Implemented cost_surface.rs: CostSurfaceAnalyzer maps render pipeline stages (CellMutation, BufferDiff, PresenterEmit, FramePipeline) from BaselineRecord, computing pipeline fractions, tail ratios, skip-certificate EV, dominance detection, primary concern classification, and CostComparison for cross-fixture dominance shift analysis. 13 unit tests.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-artifact-coding","extreme-software-optimization","ftui-performance-control-plane","ftui-render","performance","profiling","render"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vtor0","depends_on_id":"bd-17ar5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T19:18:42.659313101Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-vtor0","depends_on_id":"bd-760ih","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T18:58:48.358471588Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-vtor0","depends_on_id":"bd-9v2se","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T18:58:45.364818500Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-vtor0","depends_on_id":"bd-h0un4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T19:09:31.966005607Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-vtor0","depends_on_id":"bd-p8i4s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T18:58:51.366739085Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-vu0o","title":"Demo Showcase: Theme Studio / Live Palette Editor","description":"## Background

Themes are the fastest way to make a TUI feel "impossibly premium". FrankenTUI already has:
- a semantic token system (fg/bg/accent/status/priority)
- alpha-aware overlays
- WCAG-aware contrast helpers

This screen turns the theme engine into an interactive instrument:
- inspect presets
- edit palette values live
- validate contrast
- export for real terminal emulators

## UX / Core Interactions

- Preset list (Cyberpunk Aurora, Darcula, Lumen Light, Nordic Frost, plus any future presets).
- Token inspector:
  - show each semantic token swatch
  - show computed contrast ratio for fg/bg pairs
- Live editor:
  - adjust hue/chroma/lightness or RGB
  - lock constraints (e.g., "keep WCAG AA")
  - preview immediately across the app

Suggested keybindings:
- Ctrl+T: cycle presets (global convention)
- Enter: edit selected token
- Esc: cancel edit
- E: export

## Export Targets (Impressive + Practical)

- Export current theme to:
  - FrankenTUI theme JSON (for reproducible bug reports)
  - Ghostty theme config snippet (user-visible win)
  - optional: other formats later

## Architecture Notes

- Core theme logic must live in library code (ftui-style / ftui-extras::theme), not in the demo.
- Theme Studio is a UI over that API.
- Cache derived semantic styles (semantic swatches) per theme to keep toggles O(1).

## Acceptance Criteria

- Live updates are immediate (no restart) and deterministic.
- Contrast validation is shown in the UI and enforced in tests.
- Export produces stable output with a versioned schema.

Additionally (project-wide standards):
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode
- Visual rigor: enforce contrast bounds + deterministic rendering under fixed seeds; include snapshot fixtures and log theme IDs

## Alien Artifact Upgrades (Concrete)

- Constraint-based palette editing:
  - treat "edit a token" as a constrained optimization problem in perceptual space (OKLCH)
  - enforce minimum contrast constraints while minimizing perceptual delta from the user's intent
  - surface a tiny evidence ledger when the solver adjusts the requested color

## Extreme Optimization

- Theme switching and editing must not allocate on the hot path.
- Add a toggle-latency regression test (bd-vu0o.2).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:30:46.962183681Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T18:51:01.473071569Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T18:51:01.473045390Z","close_reason":"All subtasks closed, theme_studio.rs implemented (60KB), benches and snapshot tests exist","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo","theme","ux"],"comments":[{"id":997,"issue_id":"bd-vu0o","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Scope
- Live editing of theme tokens with immediate UI preview.
- Toggle between presets and custom adjustments.

Testing

- PTY E2E tests with verbose logging for palette changes.
